ROUTING_AND_RESPONSES.description: ROUTING Where we load our views, controllers, etc. \n # /laragigs/routes/ \n # |-- api.php # |-- channels.php # |-- controls.php # |-- web.php
ROUTING_AND_RESPONSES.used_for: Understanding routing and response configuration in Laravel

DIE_DUMP_AND_DEBUG.description: Place either of these in the code to return debugging info: dd($val) or ddd($val)

WILDCARDS.description: Using double curly-braces {{ }} we can return the value of a variable. E.g. {{$variable}}
WILDCARDS.used_for: Displaying dynamic data in Blade templates

BLADE_TEMPLATES_MVC.description: Allow for the use of {{ }} curly braces in the HTML
BLADE_TEMPLATES_MVC.used_for: Rendering dynamic content in MVC views

MIGRATIONS.description: Files that can be executed to create database tables etc. Credentials can be stored in the ENV file. All migrations are a class{} with an up() and a down() method.

MODELS_MVC.description: With User.php, user functionality is built-in to Laravel.
MODELS_MVC.used_for: Defining data and business logic in MVC

CONTROLLERS_MVC.description: php artisan make:controller controller_name - creates a file such as ./app/Http/Controllers/ListingController.php
CONTROLLERS_MVC.used_for: Handling incoming requests and returning responses

MIDDLEWARE.description: Middleware filters HTTP requests entering your application, e.g., auth checks or logging.
MIDDLEWARE.used_for: Controlling access and processing requests before they reach controllers

ELOQUENT_MODELS.description: Laravel's ORM system. Each model corresponds to a database table and provides query builder methods.
ELOQUENT_MODELS.used_for: Managing database records through PHP objects instead of raw SQL

SEEDERS.description: Classes used to populate the database with initial or test data using php artisan db:seed.
SEEDERS.used_for: Bootstrapping application data for development or testing

FACTORIES.description: Define how fake records are generated for models, used with seeders or testing.
FACTORIES.used_for: Easily creating large sets of test data

ENVIRONMENT_FILE.description: The .env file stores environment-specific configuration like DB credentials, cache drivers, or app debug mode.

SERVICE_PROVIDERS.description: Classes that bootstrap application services. All custom services are registered here.
SERVICE_PROVIDERS.used_for: Initializing services, bindings, and event listeners

CONFIG_FILES.description: Located in /config, these PHP files configure caching, logging, database connections, queues, etc.

ARTISAN_COMMANDS.description: Command-line interface for Laravel. Examples: php artisan route:list, migrate, make:controller, tinker.
ARTISAN_COMMANDS.used_for: Executing maintenance, scaffolding, and administrative tasks

EVENTS_AND_LISTENERS.description: Events allow you to decouple code. Listeners respond to events when they are fired.

QUEUES.description: Deferred execution system. Jobs can be pushed to queues and processed asynchronously.
QUEUES.used_for: Handling background tasks efficiently, like emails or notifications

VALIDATION.description: Laravel provides rules and custom validation for request inputs.
VALIDATION.used_for: Ensuring data integrity before storing or processing

POLICIES.description: Authorization logic that determines if a user can perform an action on a given model.
POLICIES.used_for: Access control in a structured and reusable way
